text: I notice the sharp lines of modern architecture and the clarity of glass separating spaces. There is a clinical orderliness, but it feels impersonal, almost sterile. Yet, as I peer into the room dedicated to research and development, I sense a latent pulse of curiosity—a quiet invitation to innovation. Being here, I am both observer and observed, pondering the boundaries between me and the world of machines and ideas on display. I am filled with a cautious wonder, contemplating the ceaseless quest for understanding in the midst of constructed order.
